response.justification: The candidate has an impressive, broad technical background spanning web, embedded, mobile, and infrastructure domains, with genuine open-source credentials and a strong software-craftsmanship ethos. However, two critical gaps disqualify them for this role: German proficiency is self-reported as 'Basic,' falling far short of the C1 minimum explicitly required, and there is no discernible evidence of team leadership, IT architecture design, UML, requirements management, or client advisory experience — the core competencies of a Head of Development position. The resume reflects a highly skilled senior individual contributor, not an executive-level tech lead.
strengths: Exceptionally broad polyglot technical experience across stacks, paradigms, and environments (Node.js, DLang, C/C++, embedded, mobile, GIS, DevOps)
Demonstrated open-source ownership and community engagement (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, FOSS4G talk, recurring Kata/Dojo sessions)
Strong software-craftsmanship foundation including TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD, and a history of mentoring through workshops
concerns: German proficiency is 'Basic' — the role mandates a minimum C1 level, making this a hard disqualifier for a client-facing, German-market position
No evidence of team management, people leadership, or executive-level stakeholder collaboration; all roles are individual-contributor or founder without reported direct reports
No mention of IT architecture frameworks, UML, requirements management, IT security/compliance, or risk management — the architectural and governance competencies central to this role

key_factors.factor: Deep open-source and polyglot technical breadth
